original_hypothesis.title: Withdraw cycle can be stalled or partially skipped if ManagedNFTs are modified mid-cycle, causing revenue collection DoS
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If `withdrawFromManagedNFTs()` is being processed in batches (nextWithdrawal > 0), and the owner calls `addManagedNFT` or releases NFTs (or otherwise modifies ManagedNFTs ordering/length), `nextWithdrawal` can become inconsistent and the withdrawal cycle may never reach `nextWithdrawal == ManagedNFTs.length`, preventing reset to 0 and stalling future withdrawals.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Revenue aggregation is central. A stuck withdrawal cursor blocks revenue collection into the ERC20 contract, which can cascade into distribution failures and operational downtime. Although owner-triggered, this is a realistic incident pattern during portfolio management.
deployment_assumptions: Operations use batched withdrawals (not always full `withdrawFromAllManagedNFTs`) due to gas constraints
Owner performs portfolio changes (add/release) while a withdrawal batch is in progress
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Hardhat scripts call `withdrawFromAllManagedNFTs()` (full cycle). No production ops runbook provided for partial batching and portfolio updates.

proof_of_signal.pattern: withdrawFromManagedNFTs(): sets nextWithdrawal=i; only resets when nextWithdrawal == ManagedNFTs.length; ManagedNFTs can be modified by owner via add/release without adjusting nextWithdrawal

preconditions: Withdrawal is executed in multiple calls so that nextWithdrawal > 0 between transactions
Owner modifies ManagedNFTs (add or release) before nextWithdrawal resets to 0
At least one ManagedNFT holds revenue requiring periodic withdrawal

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Cursor-based iteration assumes array immutability across batches but no guard enforces it.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Yes (owner portfolio actions) but can be accidental under real operations.

operational_questions: Do ops ever run withdrawals in partial batches on mainnet due to gas?
Is there an ops control preventing add/release during ongoing withdrawal cycles?
Is there monitoring that detects a stuck nextWithdrawal cursor?
validation_requirements: Test: call withdrawFromManagedNFTs(1) to set nextWithdrawal>0, then addManagedNFT/release, then attempt to finish cycle; observe cursor behavior
Check for conditions where nextWithdrawal can exceed ManagedNFTs.length and never reset

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Start batched withdrawals so nextWithdrawal is non-zero.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Observe skipped withdrawals or failure to reset nextWithdrawal, blocking future cycles.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Revenue not collected; distributions may underpay or fail due to missing funds; operational confusion
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium (may require manual remediation via custom transactions or redeploy)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: WithdrawalStarted emitted but no WithdrawalFinished for long periods
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Some ManagedNFTs never emit Withdrawal events over multiple cycles
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Portfolio management txs interleaved with withdrawal batches

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
